english: pf. bskums (bskums), ft. bskum (bskum), imp. skum(s) (skum(s)) to contract, to draw in, e.g. the leg.

also skur-klán (skur klan), skur-žús (skur zhus) abuse, occasionally blasphemy; skur-°debs-pa (skur 'debs pa), byed-pa (byed pa), smra-ba (smra ba) to abuse, viz.: persons to whom respect is due, esp. holy men or things, e.g. °págs-pa-la ('phags pa la) the venerable Dzl.; dkon-mćóg (dkon mchog) ysum (gsum) mi (mi) bdén-par (bden par) ltá-žiṅ (lta zhing) skúr-pa (skur pa) °débspa ('debs pa) to blaspheme by denying the 'Three Most Precious' Thgy. sgro-skúr (sgro skur) v. sgro (sgro).

I. pf. skur (skur), at the end of a sentence skúr-ro (skur ro), sometimes for skúr-pa (skur pa) °debs-pa ('debs pa) Mil. — II. pf. ft. & imp. bskur (bskur), pf. at the end of a sentence bskur-to (bskur to) 1. to send, to transmit, e.g. news, objects, also an army, but not a messenger; mdún-du (mdun du) skúr-ba (skur ba) to send on in advance, to have carried before, e.g. a banner; skur (skur) ynaṅ (gnang) mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) resp.: to be pleased to send. — 2. to give, hand over, deliver, consign, give in charge, commit, e.g. an army to a general; dbaṅ (dbang) skúr-ba (skur ba) to invest with power, to authorize, ji (ji) dgá-bar (dga' bar) gyíd-du (gyid du) dbaṅ (dbang) skúr (skur) čig (cig) give me power, permission, to do what I like Dzl.; rgyál-por (rgyal por) dbaṅ (dbang) skúrba (skur ba) to authorize somebody to be a ruler, to appoint, create, designate as king. The ceremony observed in such a case is a kind of anointing or baptism, pouring holy water on the crown of the head, spyi-bo-nas (spyi bo nas) dbaṅ (dbang) skúr-ba (skur ba), and as supernatural powers are supposed to be active during this process, dbaṅ (dbang) skúr-ba (skur ba) means also: to bless, consecrate, endow with miraculous power; esp. four mystical powers of meditation are imparted in this way.

pf. bskul (bskul), at the end of a sentence bskúl-to (bskul to), Ssk. चोद्, to exhort, admonish, enjoin, mi (mi) žig (zhig) las (las) byéd-par (byed par) a person to do a thing; to appoint, mi (mi) žig (zhig) lás-la (las la), in the same sense; to impose, mi (mi) žig-la (zhig la) las (las), work on somebody, — perh. a mere provincialism; deï (de'i) tsig-gis (tshig gis) bskúl-nas (bskul nas) induced by his words; rnám-šes (rnam shes) las (las) daṅ (dang) nyon-móṅs-kyis (nyon mongs kyis) bskúl-nas (bskul nas) the (departed) soul urged on, influenced, driven, by its former works and sins S.g.; lha-srín (lha srin) mčod (mchod) skul (skul) kyaṅ (kyang) though I tried to determine, to bring round, the gods and the evil spirits by sacrifices Pth.; glíṅ (gling) sogs (sogs) drág-tu (drag tu) skúl-šiṅ (skul shing) flutes and other (instruments) calling, resounding, fortissimo and so animating the actors; *yid (yid) skúl-če* (skul ce) W. to remind, admonish; *žaṅ* (zhang) (for yčaṅ (gchang)) *skúl-če* (skul ce) to rouse by shaking. — bskúl-ba (bskul ba) and more frq. bskul-ma (bskul ma) exhortation, admonition; bskúl-ma (bskul ma) °débs-pa ('debs pa), C. also skúl-rgyag-pa (skul rgyag pa), skul-čág (skul lcag) byéd-pa (byed pa) Mil. nt. to admonish, exhort. — *skúl-kan* (skul mkhan) W. overseer.

vulg. skye (skye), seld. skya (skya), neck, throat, frq.; neck of a bottle Cs.; *skye (skye) tsir (tsir) túṅ-če (tung ce), *kyíg-če (khyig ce), sdám-če* (sdam ce) W. to choke, strangle, *skyé-la (skye la) túg-pa (tug pa) tág-na (tag na) sád-če* (sad ce) id.; ske (ske) yčód-pa (gcod pa), ytúb-pa (gtub pa), °brég-pa ('breg pa) to behead, slaughter; sker (sker) °tám-pa ('tham pa) to seize by the throat, to worry Sch.; sker (sker) dógs-pa (dogs pa) to tie round the neck e.g. an amulet; ske-°Kór (ske 'khor) necklace Schr.; ske-čá (ske cha) ornament for the neck, necklace Mil.; ske-stóṅ (ske stong) Med., Sch.: cavity of the throat; ske-rmá (ske rma) Sch.: a wound of the throat, a jugular gland that has opened.
Wdṅ., Ssk. राजिका Sinapis ramosa, black mustard; mustard seed, a grain of m. s.

I. vb. pf. bskams (bskams), ft. bskam (bskam), imp. skom(s) (skom(s)) to make dry, lean, meagre; to dry up; exsiccate. — II. adj., also ském-po (skem po), dry, dried up; meagre. — skem-byéd (skem byed) a demon that causes drought Lt. — skem-nád (skem nad) Bhar. consumption.
pf. (b)skos ((b)skos), ft. bsko (bsko), imp. skos (skos) 1. to appoint, nominate, commission, charge a person, lás-su (las su) with a work Dzl., much more freq.: rgyál-por (rgyal por),
